verified.notes: Utah Medicaid has multiple programs; no senior-specific (55+) thresholds or asset limits found in official sources for 2026. General expansion targets ages 19-64 with income limits only. Assets mentioned in non-official source as $2000 but no .gov confirmation.

programs.programName: Utah PACE (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly)

verified.notes: PACE (Program of All-Inclusive Care for the Elderly) operates in 33 states but Utah is NOT listed as a participating state as of February 2026. Search results explicitly state: 'States without a PACE program: Alaska, Arizona, Connecticut, Georgia, Hawaii, Idaho, Maine, Minnesota, Mississippi, Montana, Nevada, New Hampshire, South Dakota, Utah, Vermont, West Virginia, and Wyoming.' Utah has C-PACE (Commercial Property Assessed Clean Energy), which is a different program for commercial/industrial property energy efficiency financing, not a senior care program. No Utah PACE program for seniors exists to fact-check. Age requirement (55+) is the national PACE standard but does not apply in Utah.

programs.programName: Utah SHIP (State Health Insurance Assistance Program)

verified.notes: SHIP provides free counseling to Medicare-eligible seniors (age 65+) and caregivers with no income or asset tests found in sources

programs.notes: SHIP provides free counseling to Medicare-eligible seniors (age 65+) and caregivers with no income or asset tests found in sources

verified.notes: Age requirement is 60+ for seniors. Phone number is for Utah County (primary service area); Wasatch County (435-654-4920) and Summit County (801-229-3802) also served. Program requires homebound status in addition to age. No income or asset limits found in search results; program may be needs-based but specific thresholds not documented in retrieved sources.

verified.notes: Age requirement (65+) confirmed from official Utah DHHS Medicaid source. Income and asset limits not disclosed in available search results; program eligibility is based on functional need (Nursing Facility Level of Care) assessed via MDS-HC tool, not financial means testing. Phone number is for New Choices Waiver program; Aging Waiver-specific contact not found in search results. Recommend contacting local Area Agency on Aging for complete eligibility details.
